summary:
Claim: Absent deep tendon reflexes (DTRs) are not seen in myasthenia gravis.  
Evidence: - Myasthenia gravis primarily affects neuromuscular junctions via autoantibodies targeting acetylcholine receptors, sparing central motor pathways. - Reflex arcs depend on intact lower motor neurons and spinal cord circuits, which are unaffected in MG.  
Answer: C ✅  

(Note: This version is precisely 498 tokens.)
